title: UK Open Banking Vocabulary
description: Terms, operations, and data types from the UK Open Banking API specifications v4.0.1

operations.summary: Get Direct Debits for an AccountId
Get Scheduled Payments for an AccountId
Get Standing Orders for an AccountId
Get Statement by StatementId for an AccountId
Get Statement file by StatementId for an AccountId
Get Statement Transactions for an AccountId
Confirm availability of funds for a Domestic Payment
Get details of a Domestic Payment
Create a Domestic Scheduled Payment Consent
Get a Domestic Scheduled Payment Consent
Get details of a Domestic Scheduled Payment
Create a Domestic Standing Order Consent
Get a Domestic Standing Order Consent
Get details of a Domestic Standing Order Payment
Get a File Payment Consent's Uploaded File
Upload a File for a ConsentId
Get a File Payment by FilePaymentId
Get payment details for a File Payment
Get a File Payment's Report File
Confirm Funds Availability for an International Payment
Get details of an International Payment
Create an International Scheduled Payment Consent
Get an International Scheduled Payment Consent
Confirm Funds Availability for an International Scheduled Payment
Get details of an International Scheduled Payment
Create an International Standing Order Consent
Get an International Standing Order Consent
Get details of an International Standing Order Payment
Replace an existing domestic VRP consent
Update an existing domestic VRP consent
Confirm Funds Availability for a Domestic VRP
Get details of a Domestic VRP Payment

terms.name: OBIntermediaryAgent
terms.description: The intermediary agent associated with this transaction.

terms.name: ExternalCreditorReferenceType1Code
terms.description: Specifies the type of creditor reference as published in an external creditor reference type code set. For more information see `ExternalCreditorReferenceType1Code` in *ISO_External_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: ExternalDocumentType1Code
terms.description: Specifies the document type as published in an external document type code list. For more information see `ExternalDocumentType1Code` in *ISO_External_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBRemittanceInformation2
terms.description: Information supplied to enable the matching of an entry with the items that the transfer is intended to settle, such as commercial invoices in an accounts' receivable system

terms.name: OBUltimateCreditor1
terms.description: Ultimate party to which an amount of money is due.

terms.name: OBUltimateDebtor1
terms.description: Ultimate party that owes an amount of money to the (ultimate) creditor.

terms.name: OBFrequency6
terms.description: Regularity with which credit transfer instructions are to be created and processed

terms.name: PointInTime
terms.description: Exact2NumericText - Further information on the exact point in time the event should take place. Specifies a frequency in terms of an exact point in time or moment within a specified frequency type. Note: should not be used alongside `CountPerPeriod`.

terms.name: OBFrequency6Code
terms.description: For a full list of values see `OBFrequency6Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBProxy1
terms.description: Specifies an alternate assumed name for the identification of the account.

terms.name: ExternalProxyAccountType1Code
terms.description:
Specifies the external proxy account type code, as published in the proxy account type external code set.
For more information and a full list of values see `ExternalProxyAccountType1Code` in *ISO_External_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: AccountId
terms.description: A unique and immutable identifier used to identify the account resource. This identifier has no meaning to the account owner.

terms.name: ActiveOrHistoricCurrencyCode_0
terms.description:
Identification of the currency in which the account is held. 
Usage: Currency should only be used in case one and the same account number covers several currencies
and the initiating party needs to identify which currency needs to be used for settlement on the account.

terms.name: ActiveOrHistoricCurrencyCode_1
terms.description: A code allocated to a currency by a Maintenance Agency under an international identification scheme, as described in the latest edition of the international standard ISO 4217 "Codes for the representation of currencies and funds".

terms.name: AddressLine
terms.description: Information that locates and identifies a specific address for a transaction entry, that is presented in free format text.

terms.name: BeneficiaryId
terms.description: A unique and immutable identifier used to identify the beneficiary resource. This identifier has no meaning to the account owner.

terms.name: BookingDateTime
terms.description:
Date and time when a transaction entry is posted to an account on the account servicer's books.
Usage: Booking date is the expected booking date, unless the status is booked, in which case it is the actual booking date. All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. 
All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone. An example is below:
2017-04-05T10:43:07+00:00

terms.name: BuildingName
terms.description: Name of a referenced building.

terms.name: BuildingNumber
terms.description: Number that identifies the position of a building on a street.

terms.name: CareOf
terms.description: The 'care of' address is used whenever sending mail to a person or organisation who does not actually live or work at the address. They will receive the mail for the individual.

terms.name: CountryCode
terms.description: Nation with its own government, occupying a particular territory.

terms.name: CountrySubDivision
terms.description: Identifies a subdivision of a country eg, state, region, county.

terms.name: CreationDateTime
terms.description:
Date and time at which the resource was created. All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. 
All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone. An example is below:
2017-04-05T10:43:07+00:00

terms.name: Date
terms.description:
Date and time associated with the date time type. All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date format. 
 An example is below:
2017-04-05

terms.name: DateTime
terms.description:
Date and time associated with the date time type. All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. 
All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone. An example is below:
2017-04-05T10:43:07+00:00

terms.name: DebtorReference
terms.description: A reference value provided by the PSU to the PISP while setting up the scheduled payment.

terms.name: Description_0
terms.description: Specifies the description of the account type.

terms.name: Description_1
terms.description: Description that may be available for the statement fee.

terms.name: Description_2
terms.description: Description that may be available for the statement interest.

terms.name: Description_3
terms.description: Description to describe the purpose of the code

terms.name: DirectDebitId
terms.description: A unique and immutable identifier used to identify the direct debit resource. This identifier has no meaning to the account owner.

terms.name: DistrictName
terms.description: Number that of the regional area, known as a district, which forms part of an address

terms.name: EmailAddress
terms.description: Address for electronic mail (e-mail).

terms.name: EndDateTime
terms.description:
Date and time at which the statement period ends. All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. 
All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone. An example is below:
2017-04-05T10:43:07+00:00

terms.name: FinalPaymentDateTime
terms.description:
The date on which the final payment for a Standing Order schedule will be made. All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. 
All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone. An example is below:
2017-04-05T10:43:07+00:00

terms.name: FirstPaymentDateTime
terms.description:
The date on which the first payment for a Standing Order schedule will be made. All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. 
All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone. An example is below:
2017-04-05T10:43:07+00:00

terms.name: Floor
terms.description: Number that identifies the level within a building

terms.name: Frequency_1
terms.description:
Individual Definitions:
NotKnown - Not Known
EvryDay - Every day
EvryWorkgDay - Every working day
IntrvlDay - An interval specified in number of calendar days (02 to 31)
IntrvlWkDay - An interval specified in weeks (01 to 09), and the day within the week (01 to 07)
WkInMnthDay - A monthly interval, specifying the week of the month (01 to 05) and day within the week (01 to 07)
IntrvlMnthDay - An interval specified in months (between 01 to 06, 12, 24), specifying the day within the month (-05 to -01, 01 to 31)
QtrDay - Quarterly (either ENGLISH, SCOTTISH, or RECEIVED)
ENGLISH = Paid on the 25th March, 24th June, 29th September and 25th December.
SCOTTISH = Paid on the 2nd February, 15th May, 1st August and 11th November.
RECEIVED = Paid on the 20th March, 19th June, 24th September and 20th December.
Individual Patterns:
NotKnown (ScheduleCode)
EvryDay (ScheduleCode)
EvryWorkgDay (ScheduleCode)
IntrvlDay:NoOfDay (ScheduleCode + NoOfDay)
IntrvlWkDay:IntervalInWeeks:DayInWeek (ScheduleCode + IntervalInWeeks + DayInWeek)
WkInMnthDay:WeekInMonth:DayInWeek (ScheduleCode + WeekInMonth + DayInWeek)
IntrvlMnthDay:IntervalInMonths:DayInMonth (ScheduleCode + IntervalInMonths + DayInMonth)
QtrDay: + either (ENGLISH, SCOTTISH or RECEIVED) ScheduleCode + QuarterDay
The regular expression for this element combines five smaller versions for each permitted pattern. To aid legibility - the components are presented individually here:
NotKnown
EvryDay
EvryWorkgDay
IntrvlDay:((0[2-9])|([1-2][0-9])|3[0-1])
IntrvlWkDay:0[1-9]:0[1-7]
WkInMnthDay:0[1-5]:0[1-7]
IntrvlMnthDay:(0[1-6]|12|24):(-0[1-5]|0[1-9]|[12][0-9]|3[01])
QtrDay:(ENGLISH|SCOTTISH|RECEIVED)
Full Regular Expression:
^(NotKnown)$|^(EvryDay)$|^(EvryWorkgDay)$|^(IntrvlDay:((0[2-9])|([1-2][0-9])|3[0-1]))$|^(IntrvlWkDay:0[1-9]:0[1-7])$|^(WkInMnthDay:0[1-5]:0[1-7])$|^(IntrvlMnthDay:(0[1-6]|12|24):(-0[1-5]|0[1-9]|[12][0-9]|3[01]))$|^(QtrDay:(ENGLISH|SCOTTISH|RECEIVED))$

terms.name: FullLegalName
terms.description: The full legal name of the party.

terms.name: ISODateTime
terms.description:
All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. 
All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone. An example is below:
2017-04-05T10:43:07+00:00

terms.name: Identification_0
terms.description: Identification assigned by an institution to identify an account. This identification is known by the account owner.

terms.name: Identification_1
terms.description: Unique and unambiguous identification of the servicing institution.

terms.name: Identification_2
terms.description: Unique and unambiguous identification of a financial institution or a branch of a financial institution.

terms.name: Identification_3
terms.description: Identification of the organisation issuing the invoice, when it is different from the creditor or ultimate creditor

terms.name: Identification_4
terms.description: Identification of the party to whom an invoice is issued, when it is different from the debtor or ultimate debtor.

terms.name: LastPaymentDateTime
terms.description:
The date on which the last (most recent) payment for a Standing Order schedule was made. All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. 
All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone. An example is below:
2017-04-05T10:43:07+00:00

terms.name: LEI
terms.description: Legal entity identification as an alternate identification for a party. Legal Entity Identifier is a code allocated to a party as described in ISO 17442 "Financial Services - Legal Entity Identifier (LEI)".

terms.name: Links
terms.description: Links relevant to the payload

terms.name: MandateIdentification
terms.description: Direct Debit reference. For AUDDIS service users provide Core Reference. For non AUDDIS service users provide Core reference if possible or last used reference.

terms.name: MaturityDate
terms.description:
Maturity date of the account. All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. 
All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone. An example is below:
2017-04-05T10:43:07+00:00

terms.name: Meta
terms.description: Meta Data relevant to the payload

terms.name: Name_0
terms.description:
The account name is the name or names of the account owner(s) represented at an account level, as displayed by the ASPSP's online channels.
Note, the account name is not the product name or the nickname of the account.

terms.name: Name_1
terms.description: Name by which an agent is known and which is usually used to identify that agent.

terms.name: Name_2
terms.description: Name of Service User.

terms.name: Name_3
terms.description: Name by which a party is known and which is usually used to identify that party.

terms.name: Name_4
terms.description: Long name associated with the code

terms.name: NextPaymentDateTime
terms.description:
The date on which the next payment for a Standing Order schedule will be made. All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. 
All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone. An example is below:
2017-04-05T10:43:07+00:00

terms.name: Nickname
terms.description: The nickname of the account, assigned by the account owner in order to provide an additional means of identification of the account.

terms.name: NumberOfPayments
terms.description: Number of the payments that will be made in completing this frequency sequence including any executed since the sequence start date.

terms.name: Number_0
terms.description: Indicates whether the advertised overdraft rate is guaranteed to be offered to a borrower by the bank e.g. if it s part of a government scheme, or whether the rate may vary dependent on the applicant s circumstances.

terms.name: Number_1
terms.description: fee/charges are captured dependent on the number of occurrences rather than capped at a particular amount

terms.name: OBAccount6
terms.description:
Unambiguous identification of the account to which credit and debit entries are made. The following fields are optional only for accounts that are switched:

  * Data.Currency  
  * Data.AccountCategory  
  * Data.AccountTypeCode

For all other accounts, the fields must be populated by the ASPSP.

terms.name: OBAccount6Basic
terms.description: Unambiguous identification of the account to which credit and debit entries are made.

terms.name: OBAccount6Detail
terms.description: Unambiguous identification of the account to which credit and debit entries are made.

terms.name: OBInternalAccountStatus1Code
terms.description:
Specifies the status of account resource in code form.
For a full list of enumeration values refer to `OBInternalAccountStatus1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBActiveCurrencyAndAmount_SimpleType
terms.description: A number of monetary units specified in an active currency where the unit of currency is explicit and compliant with ISO 4217.

terms.name: OBActiveOrHistoricCurrencyAndAmount_0
terms.description: The amount of the most recent direct debit collection.

terms.name: OBActiveOrHistoricCurrencyAndAmount_1
terms.description:
Amount of money to be moved between the debtor and creditor, before deduction of charges, expressed in the currency as ordered by the initiating party.
Usage: This amount has to be transported unchanged through the transaction chain.

terms.name: OBActiveOrHistoricCurrencyAndAmount_10
terms.description: Transaction charges to be paid by the charge bearer.

terms.name: OBActiveOrHistoricCurrencyAndAmount_11
terms.description: The amount of the last (most recent) Standing Order instruction.

terms.name: OBActiveOrHistoricCurrencyAndAmount_2
terms.description: The amount of the first Standing Order

terms.name: OBActiveOrHistoricCurrencyAndAmount_3
terms.description: The amount of the next Standing Order.

terms.name: OBActiveOrHistoricCurrencyAndAmount_4
terms.description: The amount of the final Standing Order

terms.name: OBActiveOrHistoricCurrencyAndAmount_5
terms.description: Amount of money associated with the statement benefit type.

terms.name: OBActiveOrHistoricCurrencyAndAmount_6
terms.description: Amount of money associated with the statement fee type.

terms.name: OBActiveOrHistoricCurrencyAndAmount_7
terms.description: Amount of money associated with the statement interest amount type.

terms.name: OBActiveOrHistoricCurrencyAndAmount_8
terms.description: Amount of money associated with the amount type.

terms.name: OBActiveOrHistoricCurrencyAndAmount_9
terms.description: Amount of money in the cash transaction entry.

terms.name: OBAddressType2Code
terms.description:
Identifies the nature of the postal address.
For a full set of codes see `OBAddressType2Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets).

terms.name: OBBalanceType1Code
terms.description:
Balance type, in a coded form.
For a full list of enumeration values refer to `ExternalBalanceType1Code` in *ISO_External_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets).

terms.name: OBBankTransactionCodeStructure1
terms.description: Set of elements used to fully identify the type of underlying transaction resulting in an entry.

terms.name: OBBranchAndFinancialInstitutionIdentification5_0
terms.description: Party that manages the account on behalf of the account owner, that is manages the registration and booking of entries on the account, calculates balances on the account and provides information about the account.

terms.name: OBBranchAndFinancialInstitutionIdentification5_1
terms.description:
Party that manages the account on behalf of the account owner, that is manages the registration and booking of entries on the account, calculates balances on the account and provides information about the account.
This is the servicer of the beneficiary account.

terms.name: OBBranchAndFinancialInstitutionIdentification6_0
terms.description:
Party that manages the account on behalf of the account owner, that is manages the registration and booking of entries on the account, calculates balances on the account and provides information about the account.
This is the servicer of the beneficiary account.

terms.name: OBBranchAndFinancialInstitutionIdentification6_1
terms.description: Financial institution servicing an account for the creditor.

terms.name: OBBranchAndFinancialInstitutionIdentification6_2
terms.description: Financial institution servicing an account for the debtor.

terms.name: OBCashAccount5_0
terms.description: Provides the details to identify the beneficiary account.

terms.name: OBCashAccount5_1
terms.description: Provides the details to identify the beneficiary account.

terms.name: OBCashAccount6_0
terms.description: Unambiguous identification of the account of the creditor, in the case of a debit transaction.

terms.name: OBCashAccount6_1
terms.description: Unambiguous identification of the account of the debtor, in the case of a credit transaction.

terms.name: ExternalCategoryPurpose1Code
terms.description: Enumeration of codes that outlines the type of purpose behind a transaction, payment or risk.  For all enum values see `ExternalCategoryPurpose1Code` in *ISO_External_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBExternalMandateClassification1Code
terms.description: Type of mandate instruction. For a full list of values see `OBExternalClassification1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBCommunicationMethod
terms.description: For a full list of enumeration values see `OBExternalCommunicationMethod2Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBCreditDebitCode_0
terms.description:
Indicates whether the amount is a credit or a debit. For a full list of values see `OBInternalCreditDebitCode` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)
Usage: A zero amount is considered to be a credit amount.

terms.name: OBCreditDebitCode_1
terms.description:
Indicates whether the transaction is a credit or a debit entry.

For a full list of enumeration values refer to `OBInternalCreditDebitCode` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBCreditDebitCode_2
terms.description:
Indicates whether the balance is a credit or a debit balance. Usage: A zero balance is considered to be a credit balance.
For a full list of enumeration values refer to `OBInternalCreditDebitCode` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBCurrencyExchange5
terms.description: Set of elements used to provide details on the currency exchange.

terms.name: ExternalEntryStatus1Code
terms.description:
Status of a transaction entry on the books of the account servicer.
For a full list of enumeration values refer to `ExternalEntryStatus1Code` in *ISO_External_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalTransactionMutability1Code
terms.description:
Specifies the Mutability of the Transaction record.
For a full list of enumeration values refer to `OBInternalTransactionMutability1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBErrorResponse1
terms.description: An array of detail error codes, and messages, and URLs to documentation to help remediation.

terms.name: OBInternalAccountIdentification4Code
terms.description:
Name of the identification scheme, in a coded form as published in an external list.
For a full list of enumeration values refer to `OBInternalAccountIdentification4Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalAccountRole1Code
terms.description: A party’s role with respect to the related account. For a full list refer to `OBInternalAccountRole1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBExternalAccountSubType1Code
terms.description:
Specifies the sub type of account (product family group)
For a full list of enumeration values refer to `OBExternalCashAccountType1Code` *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalAccountType1Code
terms.description:
Specifies the type of account (personal or business).
For a full list of enumeration values refer to 'OBInternalAccountType1Code' in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBExternalBalanceSubType1Code
terms.description: Amount sub type, in a coded form. Default if not specified is BCUR of the account.

terms.name: OBInternalFinancialInstitutionIdentification4Code
terms.description:
Name of the identification scheme, in a coded form as published in an external list.
For a full list of enumeration values refer to `OBInternalFinancialInstitutionIdentification4Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalSwitchStatusCode
terms.description:
Specifies the switch status for the account, in a coded form.
For a full list of enumeration values refer to `OBInternalSwitchStatusCode` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalLegalStructureType1Code
terms.description: Legal standing of the party. For a full list refer to `OBInternalLegalStructureType1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalPartyType1Code
terms.description: Party type, in a coded form. For a full list see `OBInternalPartyType1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBExternalPurpose1Code
terms.description: For a full list of enumeration values refer to `ExternalPurpose1Code` in *ISO_External_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalScheduleType1Code
terms.description: Specifies the scheduled payment date type requested. For a full list of enumeration values refer to `OBInternalScheduleType1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: ExternalMandateStatus1Code
terms.description: Specifies the status of the standing order in code form. For a full list of enumeration values refer to 'ExternalMandateStatus1Code' in *ISO_External_Codeset* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalStatementAmountType1Code
terms.description: Amount type, in a coded form.

terms.name: OBInternalStatementBenefitType1Code
terms.description: Benefit type, in a coded form. For a full list of values see `OBInternalStatementBenefitType1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalStatementDateTimeType1Code
terms.description: Date time type, in a coded form. For a full list of values see `OBInternalStatementDateTimeType1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalStatementFeeFrequency1Code
terms.description: How frequently the fee is applied to the Account. For a full list of values see `OBInternalStatementFeeFrequency1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalStatementFeeRateType1Code
terms.description: This code indicates the specific type of fee rate (e.g., AER, EAR). For a full list of values see `OBInternalStatementFeeRateType1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalStatementFeeType1Code
terms.description: Fee type, in a coded form. For a full list of values see `OBInternalStatementFeeType1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalStatementInterestFrequency1Code
terms.description: Specifies the statement fee type requested. For a full list of values see `OBInternalStatementInterestFrequency1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalStatementInterestRateType1Code
terms.description: This code specifies the type of interest (e.g., BOE Base Rate, Fixed Rate, Gross).

terms.name: OBInternalStatementInterestType1Code
terms.description: Interest amount type, in a coded form. For a full list of values see `OBInternalStatementInterestType1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalStatementRateType1Code
terms.description: Statement rate type, in a coded form. For a full list of values see `OBInternalStatementRateType1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalStatementType1Code
terms.description: Statement type, in a coded form. For a full list of values see `OBInternalStatementType1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalStatementValueType1Code
terms.description: Statement value type, in a coded form. For a full list of values see `OBInternalStatementValueType1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBFrequency2
terms.description: For a full list of enumeration values refer to `OBFrequency2Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBFrequencyPeriodType
terms.description:
Individual Definitions:
 ADHO - Adhoc 
 YEAR - Annual
DAIL - Daily
INDA - Intra Day
MNTH - Monthly
QURT - Quarterly
WEEK - Weekly

terms.name: OBFileFormat
terms.description: For a full list of enumeration values refer to `ExternalDocumentFormat1Code` in *ISO_External_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBExternalStatusReason1Code
terms.description: Low level textual error code, for all enum values see `OBExternalStatusReason1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBMandateRelatedInformation1
terms.description: Provides further details of the mandate signed between the creditor and the debtor.

terms.name: OBMerchantDetails1
terms.description: Details of the merchant involved in the transaction.

terms.name: OBPartyRelationships1
terms.description: The Party's relationships with other resources.

terms.name: OBPostalAddress7
terms.description: Information that locates and identifies a specific address, as defined by postal services.

terms.name: OBRate1_0
terms.description: Rate charged for Statement Fee (where it is charged in terms of a rate rather than an amount)

terms.name: OBRate1_1
terms.description: Rate for Statement Interest (where it is applicable in terms of a rate rather than an amount)

terms.name: OBInternalConsentStatus1Code

terms.name: OBTransactionCashBalance
terms.description: Set of elements used to define the balance as a numerical representation of the net increases and decreases in an account after a transaction entry is applied to the account.

terms.description:
The Risk section is sent by the initiating party to the ASPSP.
It is used to specify additional details for risk scoring for Account Info.

terms.description: Cap amount charged for a fee/charge

terms.name: OB_Amount1_1

terms.name: OB_Amount1_3

terms.name: OB_Amount1_4
terms.description: Cap amount charged for a fee/charge (where it is charged in terms of an amount rather than a rate)

terms.name: OB_CodeMnemonic
terms.description: The four letter Mnemonic used within an XML file to identify a code

terms.name: OB_FeeCategory1Code

terms.name: OpeningDate
terms.description:
Date on which the account and related basic services are effectively operational for the account owner. All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. 
All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone. An example is below:
2017-04-05T10:43:07+00:00

terms.name: PartyId
terms.description: A unique and immutable identifier used to identify the customer resource. This identifier has no meaning to the account owner.

terms.name: PartyNumber
terms.description: Number assigned by an agent to identify its customer.

terms.name: PhoneNumber_0
terms.description: Collection of information that identifies a phone number, as defined by telecom services.

terms.name: PhoneNumber_1
terms.description: Collection of information that identifies a mobile phone number, as defined by telecom services.

terms.name: PostBox
terms.description: Information that locates and identifies a box in a post office assigned to a person or organization, where letters for them are kept until called for.

terms.name: PostCode
terms.description: Identifier consisting of a group of letters and/or numbers that is added to a postal address to assist the sorting of mail.

terms.name: PreviousPaymentDateTime
terms.description:
Date of most recent direct debit collection. All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. 
All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone. An example is below:
2017-04-05T10:43:07+00:00

terms.name: ProprietaryBankTransactionCodeStructure1
terms.description: Set of elements to fully identify a proprietary bank transaction code.

terms.name: Rate
terms.description: Rate associated with the statement rate type.

terms.name: Reference
terms.description:
Unique reference, as assigned by the creditor, to unambiguously refer to the payment transaction.
Usage: If available, the initiating party should provide this reference in the structured remittance information, to enable reconciliation by the creditor upon receipt of the amount of money.
If the business context requires the use of a creditor reference or a payment remit identification, and only one identifier can be passed through the end-to-end chain, the creditor's reference or payment remittance identification should be quoted in the end-to-end transaction identification.

terms.name: Room
terms.description: Information that locates and identifies a room to form part of an address

terms.name: OBExtendedProprietaryBankTransactionCode
terms.description: Additional proprietary bank transaction codes used by the ASPSP for the underlying transaction

terms.name: OBInternalBeneficiaryType1Code
terms.description: Specifies the Beneficiary Type.

terms.name: ScheduledPaymentDateTime
terms.description:
The date on which the scheduled payment will be made. All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. 
All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone. An example is below:
2017-04-05T10:43:07+00:00

terms.name: ScheduledPaymentId
terms.description: A unique and immutable identifier used to identify the scheduled payment resource. This identifier has no meaning to the account owner.

terms.name: SecondaryIdentification
terms.description:
This is secondary identification of the account, as assigned by the account servicing institution. 
This can be used by building societies to additionally identify accounts with a roll number (in addition to a sort code and account number combination).

terms.name: StandingOrderId
terms.description: A unique and immutable identifier used to identify the standing order resource. This identifier has no meaning to the account owner.

terms.name: StartDateTime
terms.description:
Date and time at which the statement period starts. All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. 
All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone. An example is below:
2017-04-05T10:43:07+00:00

terms.name: StatementId
terms.description: Unique identifier for the statement resource within a servicing institution. This identifier is both unique and immutable.

terms.name: StatementReference
terms.description: Unique reference for the statement. This reference may be optionally populated if available.

terms.name: StatusUpdateDateTime
terms.description:
Date and time at which the resource status was updated. All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. 
All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone. An example is below:
2017-04-05T10:43:07+00:00

terms.name: StreetName
terms.description: Name of a street or thoroughfare.

terms.name: Time
terms.description:
Time at which assets become available to the account owner in case of a credit entry, or cease to be available to the account owner in case of a debit transaction entry.
Usage: If transaction entry status is pending and value date is present, then the value date refers to an expected/requested value date.
For transaction entries subject to availability/float and for which availability information is provided, the value date must not be used. In this case the availability component identifies the number of availability days. All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. 
All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone. An example is below:
T10:43:07+00:00

terms.name: TownName
terms.description: Name of a built-up area, with defined boundaries, and a local government.

terms.name: TransactionId
terms.description: Unique identifier for the transaction within a servicing institution. This identifier is both unique and immutable.

terms.name: TransactionInformation
terms.description:
Further details of the transaction. 
This is the transaction narrative, which is unstructured text.

terms.name: TransactionReference
terms.description: Unique reference for the transaction. This reference is optionally populated, and may as an example be the FPID in the Faster Payments context.

terms.name: UnitNumber
terms.description: Number that identifies the unit of a specific address .

terms.name: Value
terms.description: Value associated with the statement value type.

terms.name: ValueDateTime
terms.description:
Date and time at which assets become available to the account owner in case of a credit entry, or cease to be available to the account owner in case of a debit transaction entry.
Usage: If transaction entry status is pending and value date is present, then the value date refers to an expected/requested value date.
For transaction entries subject to availability/float and for which availability information is provided, the value date must not be used. In this case the availability component identifies the number of availability days. All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. 
All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone. An example is below:
2017-04-05T10:43:07+00:00

terms.name: OBEvent1
terms.description: Events.

terms.name: OBEventLink1
terms.description: Resource links to other available versions of the resource.

terms.name: OBEventNotification1
terms.description: The resource-update event.

terms.name: OBEventResourceUpdate1
terms.description: Resource-Update Event.

terms.name: OBEventSubject1
terms.description: The resource-update event.

terms.name: Version
terms.description: Version for the event notification.

terms.name: OBDomesticRefundAccount1
terms.description: Unambiguous identification of the refund account to which a refund will be made as a result of the transaction.

terms.name: ActiveOrHistoricCurrencyCode
terms.description: A code allocated to a currency by a Maintenance Agency under an international identification scheme, as described in the latest edition of the international standard ISO 4217 "Codes for the representation of currencies and funds".

terms.name: Department
terms.description: Identification of a division of a large organisation or building.

terms.name: Name
terms.description: Name by which an agent is known and which is usually used to identify that agent.

terms.name: OBActiveOrHistoricCurrencyAndAmount
terms.description: Amount of money associated with the charge type.

terms.name: OBAddressTypeCode
terms.description: Identifies the nature of the postal address.

terms.name: OBInternalChargeBearerType1Code
terms.description: Specifies which party/parties will bear the charges associated with the processing of the payment transaction. For a full list of values refer to `OBInternalChargeBearerType1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBCashAccountDebtor4
terms.description: ^ Only included in the response if `Data. ReadRefundAccount` is set to `Yes` in the consent.

terms.name: OBInternalExtendedAccountType1Code
terms.description: Specifies the extended type of account.

terms.name: OBInternalLocalInstrument1Code
terms.description:
User community specific instrument.
Usage: This element is used to specify a local instrument, local clearing option and/or further qualify the service or service level. For a full list of values refer to `OBInternalLocalInstrument1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalPaymentChargeType1Code
terms.description: Charge type, in a coded form. For a full list of values refer to `OBInternalPaymentChargeType1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: ExternalPurpose1Code
terms.description: This is a partial list, For a full list see `ExternalPurpose1Code` [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBRegulatoryAuthority2
terms.description: Entity requiring the regulatory reporting information.

terms.name: OBRegulatoryReporting1
terms.description: Information needed due to regulatory and statutory requirements

terms.name: OBRisk1
terms.description:
The Risk section is sent by the initiating party to the ASPSP.
It is used to specify additional details for risk scoring for Payments.

terms.name: OBSCASupportData1
terms.description: Supporting Data provided by TPP, when requesting SCA Exemption.

terms.name: OBStructuredRegulatoryReporting3
terms.description: Set of elements used to provide details on the regulatory reporting information.

terms.name: OBInternalConsentStatus2Code
terms.description: Specifies the status of consent resource in code form. For a full list of values refer to `OBInternalConsentStatus2Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: OBInternalConsentStatus3Code
terms.description: Specifies the status of consent resource in code form.

terms.name: SubDepartment
terms.description: Identification of a sub-division of a large organisation or building.

terms.name: ExternalPaymentTransactionStatus1Code
terms.description:
The current status of the payment resource.

For a full list of values see `ExternalPaymentTransactionStatus1Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* 
[here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: ExternalPaymentTransactionStatus2Code
terms.description:
Specifies the status of the payment order resource. 

For a full list of values see `ExternalPaymentTransactionStatus2Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* 
[here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: ExternalPaymentTransactionStatus3Code

Specifies the status of the payment information group.

For a full list of values refer to `ExternalPaymentTransactionStatus3Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* [here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets)

terms.name: ExternalPaymentTransactionStatus4Code
terms.description:
Specifies the status of the payment order resource.

See `ExternalPaymentTransactionStatus4Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* 
[here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets) for more information.

terms.name: OBVRPFundsConfirmationRequest
terms.description: The OBVRPFundsConfirmationRequest object must be used to request funds availability for a specific amount in the Debtor Account included in the VRP consents.

terms.name: OBVRPFundsConfirmationResponse
terms.description: The confirmation of funds response contains the result of a funds availability check.

terms.name: OBPAFundsAvailableResult1
terms.description: Availability result, clearly indicating the availability of funds given the Amount in the request.

terms.name: OBExternalCreditorReferenceType1Code
terms.description:
Specifies the type of creditor reference as published in an external creditor reference type code set.
External code sets can be downloaded from www.iso20022.org.
*`DISP`-Document is a dispatch advice.
*`FXDR`-Document is a pre-agreed or pre-arranged foreign exchange transaction to which the payment transaction refers.
*`PUOR`-Document is a purchase order.
*`RPIN`-Document is a linked payment instruction to which the current payment instruction is related, for example, in a cover scenario.
*`RADM`-Document is a remittance advice sent separately from the current transaction.
*`SCOR`-Document is a structured communication reference provided by the creditor to identify the referred transaction.

terms.name: OBVRPRemittanceInformation
terms.description: Information supplied to enable the matching of an entry with the items that the transfer is intended to settle, such as commercial invoices in an accounts' receivable system.

terms.name: OBExternalAccountType1Code
terms.description: Specifies the type of account (personal or business).

terms.name: OBVRPStatusReasonCode
terms.description: Reason Code provided for the status of a VRP. To be documented in the Developer Portal.

terms.name: OBVRPInteractionTypes
terms.description: Indicates interaction type, currently if customer is present or not present. If not provided the default is `OffSession` (customer is not present) when the individual VRP payment is made.

terms.name: ExternalPaymentTransactionStatus5Code
terms.description:
Specifies the status of the payment information group. 

For a full list of values refer to `ExternalPaymentTransactionStatus5Code` in *OB_Internal_CodeSet* 
[here](https://github.com/OpenBankingUK/External_Internal_CodeSets).
